meta.title: Roam — Make travel freer, wherever you go.
meta.description: A quiet eSIM that catches the signal so you don’t have to. One line, 200+ destinations. Wherever you go.

hero.eyebrowText: Make travel freer · wherever you go.
hero.subtitle: One eSIM, 200+ destinations. Roam is the quiet companion for the unfamiliar — tap a node for live coverage.
hero.deviceFootnote: Works on iPhone XS and newer · Google Pixel 3 and newer.

howItWorks.title: Three steps, gate to ground.
howItWorks.subtitle: Roam talks to the local carrier on your behalf, picks the strongest signal, and stays out of your way. You just walk.

steps.title: Pick where you’re going.
steps.body: Choose a country — or pick a regional bundle for the whole trip.

steps.title: Scan a QR.
steps.body: Roam installs as a second line in under a minute. Your number stays untouched.

steps.title: Land, and you’re on.
steps.body: It wakes the moment you connect to a local network. Top up in the app whenever.

walk.title: Signal slips, Roam catches it.
walk.subtitle: When a network goes quiet, Roam hops to another. You keep walking; we hold the line.

footer.subtitle: Roam — a quiet eSIM that just works, so an unfamiliar city starts to feel like one you know.

privacy.title: What we hold, and what we don’t.
privacy.subtitle: Roam runs on a near-zero data diet. Everything below is the short version. If you want the long version, ask us — we’ll send it.

sections.title: What we collect
sections.body: An email for your account, a device identifier to bind the eSIM profile, and anonymised data-usage counters so we can bill you correctly.

sections.title: What we don’t
sections.body: We don’t inspect your traffic. We don’t read your messages or listen to your calls. We don’t sell anything to advertisers, ever.

sections.title: Where it lives
sections.body: Encrypted at rest in EU and APAC regions. Access is logged. Backups expire after 30 days. No data ever leaves to a third country without your explicit consent.

sections.title: Your rights
sections.body: Export everything we hold about you, or delete it, from Settings → Privacy in the Roam app. We respond to requests within 7 days.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: Questions, edge cases, regulator requests: privacy@roam.example. A human reads every email.

faq.title: Quick answers.
faq.subtitle: If your question isn’t here, chat us from the Roam app — a human picks up within minutes.

items.a: Any iPhone XS or newer, Google Pixel 3 or newer, and most Samsung flagships from 2020 onward. Tap “See compatibility” in checkout to confirm yours.

items.q: Will Roam replace my home SIM?
items.a: No. Roam installs as a second line. Your home number stays active for calls, texts, and 2FA.
About a minute. Scan a QR, accept the cellular plan, done.

items.q: Can I install before I fly?
items.a: Yes — that’s the recommended path. Install at home over wifi, switch the data line on once you land.

items.q: I switched destinations mid-trip. What now?
items.a: Open the app, pick the new country, the eSIM updates in place. No reinstall.

items.q: What if I run out of data?
items.a: You can top up from the app in two taps. The new pack stacks on top of whatever’s left.
Yes. Unused data rolls over for 60 days inside the same region.
Yes on Trip and Region plans. Day plans are single-device.

items.q: What does Roam see about my usage?
items.a: Aggregate bytes per session, nothing about destinations or content. See the Privacy page for the full list.
If we can’t deliver service in your destination, we refund unused data. Standard request from Settings → Billing.

reviews.title: Notes from people on the move.
reviews.subtitle: Sample stories from early Roam travelers. Real reviews land at launch — these are the kinds of trips Roam was built for.

items.name: Lin Wei
items.quote: Landed at Haneda at midnight, had data before I cleared customs. Map worked, taxi app worked, family chat worked. That was the whole pitch.

items.name: Mei Chen
items.quote: Switched between London, Edinburgh, and Dublin without touching anything. No SIM swaps, no airport queues, no panic at 2am.

items.name: Hiro Tanaka
items.quote: Drove from LA to Seattle. Signal followed me up the coast. Tethered the laptop in two state parks where I’d have given up before.

items.name: Anya Park
items.quote: Got off the train in Florence, opened the maps app, walked to my Airbnb. No mid-trip side quests to find a SIM kiosk.

items.name: Marcus Reid
items.quote: Cheaper than the hostel-recommended local SIM, and I kept my US number for 2FA the whole way through.

items.name: Jisoo Han
items.quote: Two parents, two kids, two eSIMs. Plans shared into a Family bundle in the app. Sydney to Cairns, never a dead pocket.
